metadata: Global world cities with coordinates, country codes, admin region, capital status, population
content: Global world cities with coordinates, country codes, admin region, capital status, population CSV table listing major world cities and related attributes. Columns include city names (original and ASCII), latitude/longitude, country, ISO2/ISO3 codes, first-level administrative region (admin_name), capital classification, population, and a city identifier. The sample shows large metropolitan areas worldwide (e.g., Tokyo, Delhi, Shanghai) with numeric populations and geocoordinates suitable for mapping, geocoding, or demographic analysis. Population values appear to be floating-point numbers and some fields may be blank (e.g., non-capital cities), so missing values should be handled during analysis.

metadata: Kramabench Archeology Easy 3 Roman cities gazetteer with coordinates and chronology

metadata: ODP 967 North African humidity-aridity geochemical and dust proxy measurements (3 Myr index)
content: ODP 967 North African humidity-aridity geochemical and dust proxy measurements (3 Myr index) CSV table from the data supplement to “A 3 million year index for North African humidity/aridity…”, containing Ocean Drilling Program (ODP) Site 967 sediment core measurements. Rows list core location fields (site, hole, core, section, interval, depths), age (ky), and elemental concentrations (e.g., Al, Si, Ca, Ti, Fe, Sr, Zr, Ba; ppm). It also includes principal components (PC1, PC2), a rescaled ODP 967 dust proxy based on magnetic hematite variations, and a derived wet-dry index. Column headers appear partially generic, suggesting some parsing/formatting noise.

metadata: Regional archaeology radiocarbon determinations with calibrated BC ranges and site coordinates

metadata: Medieval conflict events dataset with years, fatalities, and time-bucket fields
content: Medieval conflict events dataset with years, fatalities, and time-bucket fields CSV table listing named conflicts with StartYear and EndYear, plus optional Fatalities. Additional derived time fields include Century and Decade (e.g., 900, 910, 920). The sample shows many European and Byzantine-related conflicts around the 10th century (e.g., Vikings, Magyars, Byzantium). Fatalities are often missing, with occasional numeric entries. Data appears to be an event index rather than detailed battle records; some conflict names include parentheses and bracketed regions, suggesting heterogeneous source naming.

metadata: Somatic mutation calls with adjacent gene annotations (mmc3 Sheet E)
content: Somatic mutation calls with adjacent gene annotations (mmc3 Sheet E) CSV table of putative somatic variants for multiple samples, listing genomic coordinates (CHROM, POS), alleles (REF, ALT), filter/status, and per-sample genotype/read-depth fields for matched NORMAL and TUMOR. The INFO field includes variant-level annotations such as allele counts/frequencies, depth, caller set (e.g., varscan, mutect, strelka), and predicted consequences (CSQ) with gene/transcript effects (e.g., missense, synonymous, upstream/downstream). This appears to represent “Mutation adjacent” entries from a supplementary sheet; some fields are densely encoded, so interpretation depends on correct parsing of INFO/CSQ.

metadata: Phosphoproteomics and proteomics fold-change statistics by gene feature (CNV-low comparison)
content: Phosphoproteomics and proteomics fold-change statistics by gene feature (CNV-low comparison) CSV table of gene-level features (e.g., specific phosphosites) with differential phosphorylation and protein abundance statistics. metadata: README sheet describing multi-omics tables for biomedical dataset S0092867420301070 mmc2
content: README sheet describing multi-omics tables for biomedical dataset S0092867420301070 mmc2 This CSV is a README table listing sheet names and brief descriptions for a multi-omics biomedical dataset (mmc2). It enumerates data modalities and processing notes, including: global proteomics, phosphoproteomics, acetylproteomics (median polished, log2 transformed), gene expression and circRNA expression (RSEM, upper-quantile normalized, log2(+1)), gene copy number variation from WGS ratios, miRNA expression (RPM, log2(+1)), gene methylation (median probe beta per gene), and somatic mutations (hg38, site-level). It functions as documentation/metadata rather than primary measurements.

metadata: Predicted neoantigens from somatic variants with netMHCpan HLA binding metrics
content: Predicted neoantigens from somatic variants with netMHCpan HLA binding metrics CSV table of predicted neoantigen peptides per sample derived from somatic variants. Each row links a variant (chromosome, coordinates, ref/alt, variant type/function, gene and mRNA transcript) to a candidate neoepitope sequence and amino-acid change positions. It includes HLA allele, netMHCpan predicted binding affinity (nM) and percentile rank, plus supporting evidence fields such as RNA variant evidence, gene expression, and protein/peptide evidence. Multiple rows may represent different transcripts, peptide lengths, or HLA types for the same variant. Some column names appear inconsistently spelled (e.g., percentile), so minor parsing uncertainty is possible.
